title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry mouth

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain salivary enzymes that stimulate saliva production, replenishing the natural balance of moisture in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and carnacic acid, have been shown to increase the production of saliva, which can help alleviate symptoms of dry mouth, including discomfort, sticky or tacky feelings, and difficulty chewing or swallowing.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Sage contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry mouth by protecting the mucous membranes from damage.
Mucilages: The mucilage in sage helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Salivary stimulating compounds: Sage contains compounds that stimulate saliva production, which can help alleviate symptoms of dry mouth such as xerostomia (dryness of the mouth).
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, alleviating dry mouth symptoms.

Salvianolic acid: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the oral mucosa, thus alleviating dry mouth and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the mouth, thereby alleviating dry mouth symptoms and promoting oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and moisturize the mouth.
Leaves: Contain antimicrobial properties that help to reduce bacteria and inflammation in the mouth, relieving dry mouth symptoms.
Leaves: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, providing relief for dry mouth sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 1:1 ratio of salvia officinalis powder to a carrier agent like cellulose or starch.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with approximately 0.5 grams of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the salivary glands. This anti-inflammatory property helps to increase saliva production, which can alleviate symptoms of dry mouth such as sticky or dry feeling in the mouth and difficulty chewing and swallowing. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system may also help to relax the muscles involved in speaking and eating, further reducing discomfort associated with dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Antimuscarinic effect: Lemon balm contains compounds that block the action of muscarinic receptors, which can help reduce saliva production and alleviate dry mouth symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing discomfort caused by dry mouth.

Gastroprotective effects: Lemon balm has been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage, which can be beneficial for individuals with dry mouth who may experience stomach upset or inflammation due to reduced saliva production.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and moisturize the oral tissues to reduce dry mouth symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the oral mucosa from damage and promote saliva production to alleviate dry mouth.

Volatile oils (e.g. linalyl acetate): A mixture of terpenes and esters that exhibit anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-related dry mouth symptoms by promoting relaxation and saliva production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and moisturize the oral cavity.
Flowers: They contain compounds like linalool and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, reducing dry mouth symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are also rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which contribute to their soothing and protective effects on the oral mucosa.
remedy_recipe: Dry 50 grams of melissa officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 50 grams of the powder in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes and then let it cool.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled mixture and seal them.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain the herb Glycyrrhiza glabra, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. This can help to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by a dry mouth, allowing for a healthier and more comfortable oral environment. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help to combat gum disease and bad breath associated with dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin acts as a natural demulcent, coating the mucous membranes and protecting them from irritants, thereby relieving dry mouth symptoms.
Licodione and its derivatives: As flavonoids, they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Asperuloside: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the mucous membranes, promoting the healing of dry, irritated tissues and alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and moisturize the oral cavity.
Leaves: They possess anti-inflammatory properties and contain antioxidants that aid in soothing dry mouth and promoting oral health.
Barks: They have been used to treat various oral health issues, including dry mouth, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and sift to remove debris.
Grind 50g of the sifted roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 100g of vegetable cellulose powder and 50g of gelatin in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into 100 empty capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 2 months before consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain Myrrhis odorata, a plant-based extract that has been traditionally used to soothe oral mucous membranes. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in sweet cicely help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth such as burning sensations, sticky saliva, and difficulty eating or speaking. By promoting healthy oral tissues, sweet cicely capsules can bring relief to individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet Cicely's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms by soothing irritated tissues and reducing pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Sweet Cicely capsules can help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry mouth.

Mucilage-rich properties: Sweet Cicely contains a high concentration of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and lubricate the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort.

Phenolic Acids (Rosmarinic acid): This phenolic acid has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes and may help stimulate saliva production, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and moisturize the mouth.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to provide relief from dry mouth symptoms through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
(No other part is commonly used, but) Buds: They are sometimes used, but their use is less documented; however, they might have similar benefits as the roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried aerial parts of myrrhis odorata in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Mix the dried material with 500 grams of gelatin in a food processor until well combined.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine and a dosage of 500 mg per capsule.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain natural compounds that stimulate saliva production. The bioactive compounds in elderberry, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, have been shown to increase salivation and improve oral hydration. By promoting a healthy balance of saliva levels, elder capsules can help alleviate symptoms of xerostomia (dry mouth), reducing the risk of tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral health issues associated with dry mouth. Additionally, elderberry's anti-inflammatory properties may also soothe and calm irritated oral tissues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the salivary glands, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elderberry capsules may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat tissues, which can alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with xerostomia (dry mouth).

Mucolytic properties: Elderberry's mucolytic properties can help break down mucus and promote its removal from the oral cavity, reducing the feeling of sticky, dry sensation often experienced with dry mouth.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with dry mouth by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the mouth, which can increase saliva production and soothe dry, irritated tissues.
Sambunigrin: As a glycoside, sambunigrin may help stimulate salivary gland secretion and promote the production of saliva, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.

Ellagic acid: This polyphenolic compound has antioxidant properties that may help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry mouth, while also promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used because they are rich in antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used because they contain tannins and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry mouth.
Buds: The buds of Sambucus nigra are used because they contain similar compounds to the leaves and flowers, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a dry day.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material for 2 days to remove any moisture.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered plant material with 2 parts of gelatin or vegetable capsules in a bowl.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that help to lubricate and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. When taken as a supplement, these mucilages can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Additionally, marshmallow capsules may also help to stimulate saliva production, further aiding in the restoration of natural moisture levels in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: The primary medicinal property of marshmallow capsules, mucilage is a thick, protective gel-like substance that coats the mouth and throat tissues, providing relief from dryness, irritation, and inflammation by locking in moisture and shielding against further damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with dry mouth conditions such as stomatitis or oral thrush, promoting a healthy environment for healing and reducing pain and discomfort.

Sothating and soothing action: The mucilage and other bioactive compounds in marshmallow capsules have a calming effect on the mucous membranes of the mouth and throat, providing quick relief from dryness, burning, and itching sensations, making it an effective remedy for dry mouth conditions.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from dry mouth symptoms.

Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid and oleanolic acid): Triterpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and promote the production of saliva to combat dry mouth.

Gallic Acid: Gallic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage and promote the production of saliva to alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of mucilages, which provide soothing and protective properties for dry mouth.
Leaves: They contain mucilages and other compounds that contribute to their soothing and moisturizing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of althaea officinalis for medicinal use in amounts of 50-100 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for 1-2 weeks at 50-60°c temperature.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent like gelatin in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill a capsule mold with the powder mixture and seal the capsules for 5-10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain the herb Calendula officinalis, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. The capsules' anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by xerostomia (dry mouth), alleviating symptoms such as dryness, stickiness, and burning sensations. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties may also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold capsules contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold capsules can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry mouth.

Mucilages and emollients: Pot marigold contains a type of carbohydrate called mucilage, which can help soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to stimulate saliva production and improve oral hydration, which can alleviate dry mouth symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically kaempferol and quercetin): As potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the oral cavity, which may contribute to relief from dry mouth.

Triterpenoids (specifically alpha-amyrin and beta-amyrin): Triterpenoids have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections that may exacerbate dry mouth, while also promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and moisturize the mouth.
Leaves: They are used because they contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote oral health.
Seeds: They are used because they contain antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that can help protect the mouth and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers for the capsules and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the flowers further in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to maintain potency.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the calendula powder and seal them immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain a soothing compound called linalool, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When ingested, this compound helps to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth. Additionally, the calming effects of English lavender also promote relaxation and reduced stress levels, both of which can contribute to increased saliva production and improved oral hydration. By addressing the underlying causes of dry mouth, these capsules provide natural relief for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mucous membranes, which can alleviate symptoms of dry mouth by promoting healthy tissue repair and regeneration.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's calming compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with dry mouth.

Mucilages and demulcent properties: The capsules' mucilage content helps protect and soothe the mucous membranes, forming a physical barrier that shields them from irritants and promotes healing, which can help alleviate symptoms of dry mouth such as burning and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a mild anxiolytic and helps reduce stress, which can be a contributing factor to dry mouth. By promoting relaxation, linalool can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the oral mucosa, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment that can help alleviate dry mouth.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and moisturize the mouth.
Leaves: They are used because they contain similar compounds to the flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, which help to calm and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth.
Stems: They are used due to their high content of mucilage, a natural substance that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers when they are free of contaminants and in optimal condition.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to maintain their potency.
Weigh 60g of the dried flowers and mix with 30g of a gelatin capsule filler.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the flower-gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain menthol, a natural ingredient that stimulates saliva production. When taken orally, peppermint oil is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the salivary glands, increasing moisture levels in the mouth. This can help alleviate symptoms of dry mouth, such as sticky or rough feeling, bad breath, and difficulty speaking or eating. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can provide temporary relief from discomfort associated with dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a natural compound found in peppermint oil, helps to stimulate saliva production by activating the trigeminal nerve, which regulates salivation. This increases moisture levels in the mouth and alleviates dryness.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which is often associated with dry mouth conditions like xerostomia. This reduction in inflammation can contribute to a sense of relief from dry mouth symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Peppermint capsules' antioxidant compounds can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry mouth conditions.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps to stimulate saliva production, thereby alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
Limonene: Limonene has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help to increase saliva flow and reduce dry mouth.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make capsules for dry mouth due to their high content of menthol, which helps to stimulate saliva production.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita are used to make capsules for dry mouth as they contain a high concentration of menthone, an antiseptic and expectorant that helps to relieve dry mouth symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are also used to make capsules for dry mouth due to their cooling and soothing properties, which help to calm dry mouth irritation and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves of mentha x piperita on a sunny morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 50g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 30 seconds.
Mix 1g of the ground powder with 4g of gelatin in a small bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 4 using a manual capsule filler and seal securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain the natural antibacterial properties of thyme, which can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth. The capsules' antioxidant-rich compounds also work to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to dryness and irritation. By promoting healthy oral flora and reducing inflammation, herbal thyme capsules can help to restore a healthy balance to the mouth, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme capsules have antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses in the mouth, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme capsules help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Salivary Stimulant Properties: Thyme contains compounds that stimulate salivation, increasing saliva production and helping to alleviate dryness and stickiness in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol acts as a natural expectorant and helps to stimulate saliva production, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and promote healthy saliva production.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the mouth from further irritation and promote saliva production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their high content of volatile oils, particularly thymol, which has antimicrobial properties that help soothe and moisturize the mouth.
Flowers: They are used for their fragrance and flavor, which can contribute to the overall sensation of oral health and comfort.
Roots: They are used as a complementary part to the leaves, as they may contain additional bioactive compounds that can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers to ensure sufficient material for encapsulation.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1:1 ratio of thymus vulgaris powder with 250 milligrams of vegetable capsule powder to create the filling.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the thymus vulgaris mixture using a manual capsule filler and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain a potent herb that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This herb, known as lemon verbena, can help to reduce inflammation in the salivary glands and promote the production of saliva, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth. Additionally, the herbal extract has antibacterial properties that help to combat bacteria that can contribute to dry mouth. As a result, taking lemon verbena capsules regularly may help to restore moisture to the mouth and relieve discomfort associated with dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage mucous membranes, including those in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy saliva flow.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, helping to alleviate dryness, soreness, and irritation associated with dry mouth conditions like xerostomia.

Sialogogue properties: Lemon verbena's sialogogue effects stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to replenish moisture in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort, while also promoting oral health by washing away bacteria and food particles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which help to stimulate saliva production and alleviate dry mouth symptoms.

Barks: The barks of lemon verbena contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve oral health, making them a valuable addition to medicinal capsules for dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality verbena officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or low-temperature dehydration for 24 hours.
Grind the dried verbena into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix one part powdered verbena with two parts fillers like rice flour or potato starch in a small bowl for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture weighing approximately 500mg each taking 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the salivary glands. This inflammation is often a contributing factor to dry mouth, also known as xerostomia. Hawthorn's ability to calm and soothe the salivary glands allows for increased saliva production, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with this condition. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress, which can further exacerbate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic flavonoids: These compounds help improve blood flow to the salivary glands, stimulating saliva production and relieving dry mouth symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Vasodilatory properties: Hawthorn's vasodilating effects help relax blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the salivary glands and stimulate saliva production, alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn plant contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that can help protect the oral cavity from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in the morning.
Clean and prepare the plant material by removing any dirt or debris and cutting it into small pieces.
Combine 500 grams of the prepared plant material with 1000 milliliters of pure water in a large pot and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass container.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with a standardized extract equivalent to 250 milligrams per capsule using the prepared liquid solution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with dry mouth because of its ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation, while its antimicrobial properties can combat infections that may contribute to dry mouth. Additionally, kansas coneflower has been shown to stimulate saliva production, which can help replenish moisture and restore a healthy balance to the oral environment. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomforts of dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Kansas Coneflower contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects on the oral mucosa, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth, as well as soothe irritated tissues.

Immune-modulating activity: Kansas Coneflower has immunomodulatory effects that can help regulate the body's immune response and prevent overactive responses that can lead to dry mouth symptoms, such as excessive salivary gland inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Seeds: The seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, relieving symptoms of dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves from your garden or local supplier in bulk.
Clean and dry the plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle until it reaches a consistent texture.
Mix the powdered plant material with silica gel as filler to achieve the desired capsule fill weight of 500mg per capsule and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.
Load the mixture into an empty capsule machine and encapsulate using size 00 capsules with a filling time of approximately 2 minutes per batch.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the salivary glands. This relaxation can increase saliva production, helping to alleviate symptoms of dry mouth such as sticky or tacky feeling in the mouth, difficulty speaking or eating, and increased risk of tooth decay. Additionally, St John's Wort capsules may also help to reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, further contributing to its beneficial effects on dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dry mouth by protecting mucous membranes from damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in St John's Wort capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the oral cavity that can cause dry mouth.

Antibacterial and antifungal activity: The phytochemicals in St John's Wort capsules exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and fungal overgrowth that may contribute to dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry mouth.
Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort contain saponins and tannins that have antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the risk of infection associated with dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried st john's wort flowers and capsules or gelatin for the filling.
Measure one part dried hypericum perforatum flowers by weight to three parts carrier material like rice flour.
Mix the dried flower blend with the carrier material in a small bowl until well combined.
Fill empty capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal securely.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or use within two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain the herb Parthenium hysterophorus, which has natural antibacterial properties that combat the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth. These bacteria can contribute to a dry, sticky sensation in the mouth and throat. By reducing bacterial activity, feverfew capsules help to restore a healthy balance of flora in the oral cavity, promoting saliva production and alleviating symptoms of dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Parasympathetic stimulation: Feverfew's parasympathetic effects can increase saliva production, helping to alleviate symptoms of dry mouth by stimulating the salivary glands.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Feverfew's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to dryness and discomfort, making it easier to produce saliva naturally.
Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidants can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe dry mouth.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Collect one pound of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and sort them for quality control purposes.
Grind two hundred grams of the sorted flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with four hundred grams of glycerin in a clean glass container to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the blend per capsule, yielding approximately seventy-five capsules total.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with dry mouth because they promote saliva production, which is essential for maintaining oral health. The herbal extract has a calming effect on the nervous system, allowing the salivary glands to function properly and produce enough saliva to keep the mouth moist. As a result, valerian capsules can help alleviate symptoms of dry mouth, such as bad breath, tooth decay, and difficulty chewing or swallowing. By promoting healthy salivation, valerian capsules contribute to overall oral well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative effects: Valerian root has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms by reducing stress-induced saliva reduction.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the mouth and throat, reducing spasms that may cause dryness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms such as burning sensations or ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry mouth.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the Valerian plant, contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to combat infections that contribute to dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and cut them into small pieces.
Mix one part of the chopped root with three parts of glycerin in a glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 hours at room temperature, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then fill sterile capsules with 400mg of the liquid extract.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain extracts of the passionflower plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This plant-based ingredient works to reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can cause dryness and discomfort. Additionally, maypop's unique composition includes flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to stimulate saliva production, restoring a healthy balance to the oral mucosa and relieving symptoms of dry mouth. As a result, herbal maypop capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing dry mouth issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dry mouth by reducing saliva production.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maypop capsules may help reduce inflammation in the salivary glands, which can lead to dry mouth symptoms such as a sticky or dry feeling in the mouth.

Soothing and protective mucilage: Maypop capsules contain a mucilaginous substance that coats and soothes the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, potentially helping to reduce dry mouth discomfort and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe dry mouth.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation in the oral cavity and provide relief from dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in bulk quantities suitable for processing.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers to remove impurities and excess moisture using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for two hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder until reaching a uniform consistency of approximately one millimeter in diameter.
Mix 1 part powdered passiflora incarnata with 2 parts carrier herb like cornstarch or cellulose at a ratio of 25 grams to 50 grams for a total of 75 grams.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend using a capsule filler machine and store them in airtight containers kept away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes that work together to stimulate saliva production. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation in the salivary glands, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms. Additionally, yarrow's ability to enhance mucous membrane health helps restore a healthy balance of oral flora, further alleviating dry mouth discomfort and promoting overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.
Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and exacerbate dry mouth conditions.

Mucilage content: Yarrow capsules contain a type of fiber called mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are used to create a natural demulcent that soothes and protects the mucous membranes, helping to relieve dry mouth symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy oral tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation method in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 100mg of gelatin capsules and fill them individually, sealing with a cork or lid, taking 2 capsules twice daily.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature protected from light and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower capsules helps with dry mouth because they contain soothing herbs that calm inflamed tissues and reduce irritation in the mouth. The anti-inflammatory properties of cornflower extract specifically target the salivary glands, stimulating their natural function to increase saliva production and alleviate xerostomia (dry mouth). Additionally, the demulcent properties of cornflower provide a protective coating to the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth. As a result, cornflower capsules offer a natural solution for relief from dry, sticky, and uncomfortable mouth conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cornflower extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the mucous membranes, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms by soothing irritated tissues.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in cornflower capsules can help combat oral infections that may contribute to dry mouth, such as Candida overgrowth or bacterial vaginosis.

Mucilages and demulcents: Cornflower contains mucilage-rich polysaccharides that form a protective film on the mucous membranes, providing lubrication and soothing relief for dry, irritated tissues in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing dry mouth symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and promote saliva production, alleviating dry mouth discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centaurea cyanus flowers at peak blooming for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 3-4 hours.
Grind the dried centaurea cyanus flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herbs with gelatin capsules at a ratio of 500mg herb to 1 capsule, filling each capsule by hand or using an automated capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months, labeling and dating for future reference.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry mouth are natural supplements designed to alleviate the symptoms of xerostomia, or dry mouth. They work by stimulating saliva production and soothing oral tissues, reducing discomfort, and preventing dental problems. Examples of herbal capsules that help with dry mouth include those containing sage, peppermint, and licorice root, which have been shown to effectively reduce symptoms. For individuals suffering from dry mouth, these capsules can improve their ability to speak, eat, and sleep comfortably, leading to a significant improvement in their overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry mouth that are treated with herbal capsules are gastrointestinal issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease, and acid reflux. Herbal supplements like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root help soothe digestive tract inflammation, reduce irritation, and promote healthy mucous production. These natural remedies can also address respiratory concerns like bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the airways.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dry mouth is a blend of Licorice root, Marshmallow root, and Slippery Elm. Licorice root helps to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes, while Marshmallow root provides anti-inflammatory properties to reduce discomfort and promote saliva production. Slippery Elm, rich in mucilages, coats and protects the oral mucosa, providing long-lasting relief from dry mouth symptoms. This combination helps to restore moisture and promote overall oral health.
